steps.title: Identify the true subject
steps.content:
The sentence begins with the prepositional phrase "Among the clubs' leaders," which is not the subject. The true subject follows: "Josephine St. Pierre Ruffin, founder of the Women's Era Club of Boston" — a single person, so the subject is singular.
  • Choice A — ‘was’: Singular past-tense verb; agrees with the singular subject "Josephine St. Pierre Ruffin." ✓
  • Choice B — ‘were’: Plural past-tense verb; does not agree with a singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘are’: Plural present-tense verb; does not agree with a singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘have been’: Plural present-perfect verb; does not agree with a singular subject. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The singular subject "Josephine St. Pierre Ruffin" requires the singular verb "was." That confirms A is correct.
